text: Managing a team requires a balance of several key skills. First, clear communication is essential—everyone should understand their roles and the team’s goals. Next, delegation is not just about assigning tasks; it’s about trusting others and giving them ownership. Regular feedback helps people improve and feel valued, so offer both praise and constructive criticism. Motivation often comes from recognizing effort and providing opportunities for growth. Finally, when conflicts arise, address them quickly and fairly, listening to all sides before finding a solution. By combining these elements, a team can work together more effectively and achieve better results.
